title: Đề luyện tập 09 - RISC-V, Cache và Pipeline
description: Đề luyện tập 09. Mỗi câu 10 điểm.

questions.title: 3-way set associative cache, điền Way theo thứ tự đưa vào
questions.prompt:
Assume the memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a 3-way set associative cache with 2-word blocks and a total size of 48 words. Assume 1 word = 64 bits.

First, fill in the cache parameters. Then use true LRU. For each reference, identify binary address, tag, set index, offset, hit/miss, and the tags in each way after the reference has been handled.

Lưu ý: Nếu một Way có nhiều dòng như T(001)=0000, T(010)=1011, mỗi dòng sẽ là một ô nhập riêng. Các dòng trong Way giữ theo thứ tự block được đưa vào way, không sort theo set index.

Sequence of word addresses:
0x24, 0xc8, 0x4b, 0x25, 0xca, 0x6e, 0xcb, 0x0a, 0x2a, 0xc9, 0x6f, 0xea, 0x4a, 0x8a

fields_intro.label: Number of blocks in the cache
Number of sets in the cache
Number of blocks in the memory
Number of tag bits in each block
Total bits required to implement the cache

fields_extra.label: After these accesses, is the cache full?

questions.solution: Block offset = 1 bit, set index = 3 bits, tag = 4 bits. Hit/Miss sequence: M, M, M, H, M, M, H, M, M, H, H, M, M, M. Cache full: no. Replacements: 4. Way entries keep physical insertion order, not sorted by set index.

questions.title: Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: sw
questions.prompt:
Consider a single-cycle RISC-V processor with add, sub, and, or, lw, sw and beq.

Let the processor execute:
sw x22, 132(x5)
at address 9140. Suppose register xi = 34i for i=1,...,31. Suppose memory[M] = M + 480.
questions.solution: x5 = 170 and x22 = 748. Effective address = 302. Store data = 748. Encoding = 0x0962A223.

questions.title: 5-stage pipeline with forwarding and hazard detection
questions.prompt:
Assume that the following code sequence is executed on the 5-stage pipelined processor with forwarding and hazard detection units shown below:
lw  x6, 0(x1)
add x7, x6, x2
sub x8, x7, x6
sw  x8, 4(x7)
or x9, x8, x6
For the first seven cycles, specify the values of control signals at A, B, C, D, E, F, and G.
questions.solution: First seven cycle matrix (A B C D E F G): 1 1 xx xx 1 x x; 1 1 xx xx 1 x x; 0 0 00 00 0 x x; 1 1 00 00 1 1 x; 1 1 01 00 1 0 1; 1 1 10 00 1 1 0; 1 1 01 10 1 1 1 The first instruction is a load, so the direct lw -> add dependency stalls early in cycle 3. Later cycles use forwarding.

questions.title: Fully associative cache với true LRU
questions.prompt:
Assume the main memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a fully associative cache with 4-word blocks and a total size of 16 words. Assume one word is 64 bits.

For the sequence below, use true LRU. For each reference, identify binary address, tag, and hit/miss.

Sequence of word addresses:
0x08, 0x09, 0x20, 0x33, 0x21, 0x47, 0x48, 0x34, 0x60, 0x49, 0x61, 0x08, 0x72, 0x73

fields_extra.label: After these accesses, is the cache full?

questions.solution: 4-word blocks => offset = 2 bit(s) and tag = upper 6 bits. Hit/Miss sequence: M, H, M, M, H, M, M, M, M, H, H, M, M, H. Cache full: yes. Replacements: 5.

questions.title: Cache parameters: 4-way set associative, write-back
questions.prompt:
Here is the cache with the following key characteristics:
■ 4-Way Set Associative Cache
■ Write policy: Write-back using write allocate with one Dirty bit per block
■ Block size is 128 bytes
■ Cache size is 128 KB
■ Memory is byte addressing with 32-bit addresses.

Fill in the following values.

fields.label: Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Blocks = 128KB/128B = 1024. Sets = 1024/4 = 256, so index = 8 bits. Offset = log2(128) = 7 bits. Tag = 32 - 8 - 7 = 17 bits. Total = 1024 × (128×8 + 17 tag + 1 valid + 1 dirty) = 1068032 bits.

questions.title: Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: or
questions.prompt:
Consider a single-cycle RISC-V processor with add, sub, and, or, lw, sw and beq.

Let the processor execute:
or x27, x9, x25
at address 10000. Suppose register xi = 46i for i=1,...,31. Suppose memory[M] = M + 360.

questions.title: Fully associative cache parameters
questions.prompt: Assume the main memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a fully associative cache with 2-word blocks and a total size of 32 words. Assume one word is 64 bits.

fields.label: Number of blocks in the cache
Number of sets in the cache
Number of blocks in the memory
Number of tag bits in each block
Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Cache blocks = 32/2 = 16. Fully associative => one set. Memory blocks = 2^8/2 = 128. Offset = log2(2) = 1 bits, so tag = 8 - 1 = 7 bits. Total = 16 × (2×64 + 7 tag + 1 valid) = 2176 bits.

questions.title: Execution time: single-cycle vs pipeline
questions.prompt:
Consider the following sequence of 32-bit RISC-V instructions:
lw  x14, 0(x4)
add x15, x14, x5
lw  x16, 8(x15)
sub x17, x16, x14
sw  x17, 12(x15)
a) Single-cycle processor clock cycle time = 1000 ps.
b) 5-stage pipelined processor clock cycle time = 250 ps, without forwarding or hazard detection units. Insert NOPs to ensure correct execution.
c) 5-stage pipelined processor clock cycle time = 250 ps, with forwarding/hazard detection units.
d) Which case gives the medium performance?

fields.label: a) Execution time on single-cycle processor
b) Number of cycles after inserting NOPs
b) Execution time without forwarding/hazard detection
c) Number of stalls/NOPs needed with forwarding/hazard detection
c) Execution time with forwarding/hazard detection

questions.solution: Single-cycle: 5×1000 = 5000 ps. Without forwarding/hazard detection: 8 NOPs => 13 instructions including NOPs => 17 cycles => 4250 ps. With forwarding/hazard detection: 2 load-use stall(s) => 11 cycles => 2750 ps. The medium performance is case b.

questions.title: Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: lw
questions.prompt:
Consider a single-cycle RISC-V processor with add, sub, and, or, lw, sw and beq.

Let the processor execute:
lw x11, 148(x23)
at address 11008. Suppose register xi = 22i for i=1,...,31. Suppose memory[M] = M + 1020.

questions.title: Cache parameters: 16-way set associative, write-back
questions.prompt:
Here is the cache with the following key characteristics:
■ 16-Way Set Associative Cache
■ Write policy: Write-back using write allocate with one Dirty bit per block
■ Block size is 64 bytes
■ Cache size is 256 KB
■ Memory is byte addressing with 32-bit addresses.

Fill in the following values.

fields.label: Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Blocks = 256KB/64B = 4096. Sets = 4096/16 = 256, so index = 8 bits. Offset = log2(64) = 6 bits. Tag = 32 - 8 - 6 = 18 bits. Total = 4096 × (64×8 + 18 tag + 1 valid + 1 dirty) = 2179072 bits.
